articles.headline: India Unveils National Green Hydrogen Mission 2.0 with Ambitious Targets for Decarbonization and Energy Security
articles.explainer:
• Phase 2.0 builds on the initial mission, setting enhanced targets for green hydrogen production capacity by 2030.
• Focuses on expanding domestic manufacturing of electrolysers and promoting green hydrogen adoption across key industrial sectors like refining, fertilizers, and steel.
• Includes new financial incentives, R&D support, and development of green hydrogen corridors and export infrastructure.
• Aims to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy independence and climate goals.
• Emphasizes skill development and job creation in the emerging green hydrogen economy.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: Energy
Environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched in 2023.
Initial target for green hydrogen production: 5 MMT by 2030.
SIGHT (Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition) Programme is a key component.
Key applications include fertilizers, refineries, steel, and heavy mobility.
Nodal Ministry: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy.
mainsPoints: Significance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and reducing import bill.
Potential for decarbonizing hard-to-abate industrial sectors.
Challenges include high production cost, infrastructure development, and water availability.
Government's role in creating an enabling policy framework, incentives, and R&D support.
India's strategic advantage due to abundant renewable energy resources.
Geopolitical implications: positioning India as a global energy exporter and leader in green technologies.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration showing a futuristic green hydrogen production plant with solar panels and wind turbines in the background.
articles.trivia: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity, resulting in zero greenhouse gas emissions.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Validity of Key Provisions in Women's Reservation Bill, Paving Way for Implementation
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, affirmed the constitutional validity of the provisions related to the Women's Reservation Bill.
• The judgment clarifies the modalities and timelines for the implementation of 33% reservation for women in Lok Sabha and state assemblies.
• Emphasizes the importance of inclusive political representation for strengthening democratic processes.
• Directs the Election Commission and relevant ministries to prepare for the necessary logistical and administrative changes.
• The ruling resolves legal ambiguities, setting a clear path for the historic legislative reform.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
Role of women and women’s organization
prelimsPoints: The Women's Reservation Bill (Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam) was passed as the 128th Constitutional Amendment Bill.
Provides for 33% reservation for women in Lok Sabha, state legislative assemblies, and the Delhi assembly.
Implementation is linked to the delimitation exercise following the next census.
The reservation will be for a period of 15 years, with the possibility of extension.
Articles 330A, 332A, and 334A were inserted/amended.
mainsPoints: Significance of women's reservation for empowering women and transforming political discourse.
Challenges in implementation, including delimitation and potential political resistance.
Debate on 'quota within quota' for SC/ST/OBC women and its implications.
Impact on the quality of governance and policy-making with increased female representation.
Role of the judiciary in upholding constitutional principles and facilitating social reforms.
Comparison with international practices of gender quotas in legislatures.
articles.imageDescription: Image depicting the Supreme Court building with a gavel, symbolizing judicial pronouncement on the Women's Reservation Bill.
articles.trivia: The idea of reserving seats for women in legislative bodies was first formally proposed in India in the mid-1990s.

articles.headline: India and Japan Forge Comprehensive Strategic Partnership on Critical Minerals and Supply Chain Resilience
articles.explainer:
• India and Japan signed a landmark agreement to establish a comprehensive strategic partnership focused on critical minerals and resilient supply chains.
• The pact aims to reduce dependence on single-source nations for key minerals essential for advanced technologies and green energy transition.
• Includes joint investments in exploration, processing, and recycling of critical minerals such as lithium, cobalt, and rare earths.
• Both nations commit to sharing technological expertise and developing sustainable supply chain practices.
• This collaboration is seen as a significant step towards enhancing economic security and strategic autonomy in the Indo-Pacific region.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Critical minerals are vital for high-tech industries, renewable energy, and defense.
Examples: Lithium (EV batteries), Cobalt (batteries), Rare Earth Elements (electronics, magnets).
India has identified 30 critical minerals for its economic development.
Japan is a major technology producer reliant on stable mineral supply.
Partnership aims to diversify sources and reduce geopolitical risks in supply chains.
mainsPoints: Significance of critical minerals for India's 'Make in India' initiative and energy transition goals.
Challenges posed by global concentration of critical mineral processing and mining.
How this partnership strengthens India's strategic autonomy and reduces vulnerability.
Role of India-Japan cooperation in balancing geopolitical influence in the Indo-Pacific.
Potential for technological transfer and joint R&D in mineral extraction and processing.
Economic implications: boosting manufacturing, creating jobs, and fostering innovation.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan superimposed over an illustration of various critical minerals and electronic components.
articles.trivia: India's first critical minerals auction for 20 blocks, including lithium and rare earth elements, was launched in 2023.

articles.headline: RBI Releases Financial Stability Report, Cautions on Global Headwinds Amidst Domestic Resilience
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) published its semi-annual Financial Stability Report (FSR), assessing the health of India's financial system.
• The report highlighted the continued resilience of the banking sector, with strong capital buffers and improved asset quality.
• It cautioned about potential global headwinds, including geopolitical tensions, commodity price volatility, and slower global growth, which could impact domestic stability.
• Emphasized the need for ongoing vigilance and robust risk management frameworks across financial institutions.
• Noted the steady growth in credit and deposits, signaling healthy economic activity, but also flagged concerns regarding rising household debt in certain segments.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
Financial sector reforms
prelimsPoints: Financial Stability Report (FSR) is published by RBI twice a year.
It reflects the collective assessment of the Sub-Committee of the Financial Stability and Development Council (FSDC) on risks to financial stability.
Key indicators include Capital to Risk-weighted Assets Ratio (CRAR), Gross Non-Performing Assets (GNPA) ratio.
FSR covers commercial banks, cooperative banks, NBFCs, and other financial institutions.
FSDC is chaired by the Union Finance Minister.
mainsPoints: Role of RBI in maintaining financial stability and macro-economic management.
Interlinkages between global economic trends and India's domestic financial health.
Challenges posed by rising household debt and implications for financial inclusion.
Importance of regulatory oversight and stress testing for banking sector resilience.
Impact of digital transformation and fintech on financial stability and evolving risks.
Government and RBI coordination in addressing systemic risks and promoting inclusive growth.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic representation of financial stability, with upward trending graphs and a strong banking sector.
articles.trivia: The Financial Stability and Development Council (FSDC) was set up in 2010 to strengthen and institutionalize the mechanism for maintaining financial stability.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Key Test of Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle Prototype
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved a significant milestone by successfully conducting a crucial test of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype.
• The test involved a precise landing experiment, demonstrating autonomous navigation, guidance, and control capabilities during re-entry.
• This technology is vital for reducing the cost of space access and enabling more frequent space missions.
• The RLV project aims to develop a two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) fully reusable launch system.
• The successful test brings India closer to developing its own space shuttle-like capabilities, bolstering its position in global space exploration.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
prelimsPoints: ISRO's RLV-TD (Reusable Launch Vehicle - Technology Demonstrator) program aims to develop reusable launch vehicles.
RLV-TD has already demonstrated hypersonic flight and autonomous landing capabilities.
It is designed to reduce the cost of launching satellites significantly.
Other countries with RLV capabilities include USA (SpaceX, NASA) and China.
Gaganyaan mission aims for human spaceflight using indigenous technology.
mainsPoints: Significance of reusable launch vehicles for India's future space missions and commercial space industry.
Economic benefits: reducing launch costs, increasing launch frequency, and enabling new space applications.
Technological challenges in developing RLVs: thermal protection systems, autonomous guidance, propulsion.
India's growing role in the global space market and its strategic implications.
Linkage with 'Make in India' and self-reliance in critical space technologies.
Potential for international collaborations and partnerships in space exploration.
articles.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle prototype descending for a landing, with the ISRO logo.
articles.trivia: The RLV-TD is designed to act as a flying testbed to evaluate various technologies, including hypersonic flight, autonomous landing, and winged re-entry.

articles.headline: Project Cheetah Success: New Cheetah Cubs Born at Kuno National Park, Boosting Conservation Efforts
articles.explainer:
• Kuno National Park has reported the birth of several new cheetah cubs, marking a significant milestone for Project Cheetah.
• The births indicate successful adaptation and breeding of the reintroduced cheetahs in their new habitat.
• This development is crucial for establishing a viable and self-sustaining cheetah population in India.
• Conservationists highlight the intensive monitoring and habitat management efforts contributing to this success.
• The project aims to restore cheetahs to India's ecosystem, enhancing biodiversity and ecological balance.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity and its conservation
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Project Cheetah was launched in 2022 to reintroduce cheetahs to India after their extinction in 1952.
Cheetahs were translocated from Namibia and South Africa to Kuno National Park in Madhya Pradesh.
Kuno National Park was chosen due to its suitable habitat and prey base.
Cheetahs are listed as 'Vulnerable' on the IUCN Red List.
India is the only country with lions, tigers, and cheetahs in its wildlife.
mainsPoints: Ecological significance of cheetah reintroduction for grassland ecosystems and predator-prey dynamics.
Challenges faced by Project Cheetah: adaptation, mortality rates, human-wildlife conflict.
Importance of community involvement and local support for conservation projects.
Role of scientific monitoring and adaptive management in wildlife reintroduction programs.
Comparison with other wildlife conservation efforts in India (e.g., Project Tiger, Project Elephant).
Ethical considerations and long-term sustainability of transboundary wildlife transfers.
articles.imageDescription: Photograph of a mother cheetah with her cubs in a grassy field at Kuno National Park.
articles.trivia: The cheetah is the fastest land animal, capable of reaching speeds of up to 120 km/h over short distances.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Overhaul of Disaster Management Framework Amidst Rising Extreme Weather Events
articles.explainer:
• A parliamentary standing committee submitted a comprehensive report recommending significant reforms in India's disaster management framework.
• The report highlights gaps in preparedness, early warning systems, and post-disaster rehabilitation efforts, especially for extreme weather events.
• Advocates for a more decentralized approach, empowering local bodies and enhancing community resilience.
• Suggests increased funding for disaster-resistant infrastructure and climate-resilient agriculture.
• Emphasizes the integration of climate change adaptation strategies into national and state disaster management plans.
gsSubTopics: Mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: Disaster Management Act was enacted in 2005.
NDMA (National Disaster Management Authority) is chaired by the Prime Minister.
NDRF (National Disaster Response Force) is a specialized force for disaster rescue operations.
India is a signatory to the Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction (2015-2030).
The 15th Finance Commission recommended allocating funds for disaster risk reduction and mitigation.
mainsPoints: Critical analysis of India's current disaster management structure and its effectiveness.
Impact of climate change on the frequency and intensity of extreme weather events in India.
Importance of multi-hazard early warning systems and last-mile connectivity.
Role of technology (AI, GIS, remote sensing) in disaster preparedness and response.
Challenges in coordination between central, state, and local agencies during disasters.
Recommendations for building a truly resilient nation against natural calamities.
articles.imageDescription: Graphic showing various natural disasters (floods, droughts, cyclones) impacting a map of India, with a protective shield over it.
articles.trivia: India has a unique geographical vulnerability, prone to over 30 types of natural disasters.

articles.headline: NEP 2020 Implementation Review Focuses on Enhancing Vocational Education Integration for Skill Development
articles.explainer:
• A mid-term review of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation highlighted significant progress in integrating vocational education.
• The review emphasized the need for further strengthening industry-academia linkages and skill development initiatives.
• Recommendations include expanding vocational courses in schools and higher education institutions, aligning with market demands.
• Focus is on making vocational education an integral part of mainstream schooling, starting from Class 6.
• Aims to equip India's youth with job-ready skills, reducing unemployment and fostering entrepreneurship.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry—the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.
One of its key aims is to integrate vocational education with mainstream education from Class 6 onwards.
It advocates for a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure.
The policy promotes multidisciplinary education and flexible learning pathways.
National Skill Development Mission was launched in 2015.
mainsPoints: Significance of vocational education in addressing India's demographic dividend and unemployment challenge.
Challenges in integrating vocational education into the existing academic structure and changing societal perceptions.
Role of technology and digital platforms in delivering vocational training.
Importance of aligning vocational curricula with industry demands and future job markets.
Impact of NEP 2020's recommendations on fostering entrepreneurship and self-employment.
Government initiatives and public-private partnerships needed for successful implementation.
articles.imageDescription: Students engaged in vocational training activities like carpentry, electronics, and culinary arts, symbolizing skill development.
articles.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 aims for 50% of learners to have exposure to vocational education by 2025.

articles.headline: Government Announces New PLI Scheme for Advanced Battery Manufacturing to Boost EV Ecosystem
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government launched a new Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme specifically for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) battery manufacturing.
• The scheme aims to attract significant investments in the domestic manufacturing of high-efficiency batteries for electric vehicles (EVs) and energy storage.
• Offers financial incentives based on sales of batteries manufactured in India, promoting local value addition.
• Expected to reduce India's import dependence for batteries and foster a robust EV ecosystem.
• This initiative aligns with India's climate commitments and goal of achieving Net-Zero emissions by 2070.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Industrial policy and its effects on industrial growth
Infrastructure: Energy
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: The PLI scheme was launched to boost domestic manufacturing in various sectors.
ACC batteries are crucial for electric vehicles and grid-scale energy storage.
India aims for 30% EV penetration in private cars, 70% in commercial vehicles, and 80% in two-wheelers by 2030.
NITI Aayog's 'India's Electric Mobility Transformation' report highlights the need for indigenous battery production.
The FAME India Scheme promotes the adoption of electric and hybrid vehicles.
mainsPoints: Significance of indigenous battery manufacturing for India's energy security and reducing import bill.
Impact of PLI scheme on attracting foreign and domestic investment in the EV sector.
Challenges in battery manufacturing: raw material sourcing, technological advancements, recycling infrastructure.
Role of EVs in achieving India's climate change targets and reducing urban pollution.
Economic benefits: job creation, technological innovation, and export potential.
Government's comprehensive strategy for promoting electric mobility (PLI, FAME, charging infrastructure).
articles.imageDescription: A sleek electric car being charged, with battery cells and manufacturing units in the background, symbolizing advanced battery production.
articles.trivia: India is the third-largest automotive market in the world, making the transition to EVs a massive undertaking.

articles.headline: India Hosts Major Multilateral Naval Exercise in Indian Ocean, Reinforcing Maritime Security Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• India successfully hosted a large-scale multilateral naval exercise in the Indian Ocean Region (IOR) involving several partner nations.
• The exercise focused on enhancing interoperability, maritime domain awareness, and coordinated responses to regional security challenges.
• Scenarios included anti-piracy operations, humanitarian assistance and disaster relief (HADR), and anti-submarine warfare.
• The drill underscores India's growing role as a net security provider and a key player in ensuring a free and open Indo-Pacific.
• Aims to strengthen diplomatic ties and foster a collaborative approach to maritime governance and security.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Security challenges and their management in border areas
keyTerms: SAGAR (Security and Growth for All in the Region)
prelimsPoints: India regularly conducts multilateral exercises like Milan, Malabar, and Samudra Shakti.
Indian Ocean Region (IOR) is strategically vital for global trade and security.
India's SAGAR doctrine emphasizes security and growth for all in the region.
HADR operations are crucial for disaster response in coastal areas.
The Indian Navy operates under the Ministry of Defence.
mainsPoints: Significance of India's maritime strategy in protecting its economic interests and securing sea lanes of communication.
Challenges to maritime security in the IOR: piracy, terrorism, illegal fishing, and geopolitical rivalries.
Role of multilateral exercises in building trust, enhancing interoperability, and projecting naval power.
India's vision for a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific and its implications for regional stability.
Contribution of naval diplomacy to India's foreign policy objectives.
Future of Indian Navy modernization and capacity building to meet evolving threats.
articles.imageDescription: Warships from various navies conducting drills in the open sea, with helicopters flying overhead, symbolizing a multilateral naval exercise.
articles.trivia: The Indian Ocean is the third-largest of the world's five oceanic divisions, covering approximately 20% of the water on Earth's surface.

articles.headline: Discussions on Uniform Civil Code (UCC) Gain Momentum as Legal Committee Submits Recommendations
articles.explainer:
• The legal committee constituted to examine the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) submitted its detailed recommendations to the government.
• The report outlines potential frameworks for a common set of laws governing personal matters like marriage, divorce, inheritance, and adoption.
• The recommendations aim to harmonize diverse personal laws currently applicable to different religious communities in India.
• Public debate on the UCC has intensified, with various stakeholders expressing views on its implications for secularism and individual rights.
• The government is expected to review the recommendations and consider legislative action, potentially leading to a significant social reform.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Social empowerment
prelimsPoints: Article 44 of the Indian Constitution states that 'The State shall endeavor to secure for the citizens a Uniform Civil Code throughout the territory of India'.
It is a Directive Principle of State Policy (DPSP), which is non-justiciable but fundamental in governance.
Goa is the only Indian state currently with a UCC, inherited from the Portuguese Civil Code.
The Law Commission of India has previously examined the UCC.
Personal laws in India include Hindu Marriage Act, Muslim Personal Law (Shariat) Application Act, Indian Christian Marriage Act, etc.
mainsPoints: Arguments for and against the implementation of a Uniform Civil Code in India.
Constitutional implications: balancing Article 44 (DPSP) with Fundamental Rights (e.g., Article 25 - Freedom of Religion).
Impact of UCC on gender justice, women's rights, and vulnerable sections of society.
Challenges in drafting and implementing a UCC in a culturally and religiously diverse nation.
Role of the judiciary and past judgments (e.g., Shah Bano case) in the UCC debate.
Political and social ramifications of such a significant legal reform.
articles.imageDescription: A balance scale weighing different religious symbols against a single book of law, representing the Uniform Civil Code.
articles.trivia: The debate over a Uniform Civil Code dates back to the colonial era, with calls for codification and reform of personal laws.

articles.headline: New Policy Framework for Sustainable Urban Development and Green Infrastructure Unveiled
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs launched a new comprehensive policy framework aimed at promoting sustainable urban development.
• The policy emphasizes green infrastructure, smart city solutions, and climate-resilient urban planning.
• Includes guidelines for integrating renewable energy, efficient waste management, and water conservation into urban projects.
• Provides financial incentives and technical assistance to states and urban local bodies for adopting sustainable practices.
• Seeks to address rapid urbanization challenges while ensuring ecological balance and improving quality of life for city dwellers.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
articles.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: Smart Cities Mission was launched in 2015 to promote sustainable and inclusive cities.
AMRUT (Atal Mission for Rejuvenation and Urban Transformation) focuses on urban infrastructure.
Swachh Bharat Mission (Urban) aims for universal sanitation coverage.
India's urban population is projected to reach over 600 million by 2030.
Green buildings use less water, optimize energy efficiency, conserve natural resources.
mainsPoints: Challenges of rapid urbanization in India: housing, sanitation, transport, pollution.
Importance of green infrastructure (e.g., urban forests, permeable pavements) in mitigating climate change impacts.
Role of technology and data analytics in developing smart and sustainable cities.
Financial and governance challenges in implementing large-scale urban development projects.
Impact of sustainable urban planning on public health, economic growth, and social equity.
Comparison with international best practices in sustainable urban design.
articles.imageDescription: A modern, green cityscape with parks, renewable energy installations, and efficient public transport, symbolizing sustainable urban development.
articles.trivia: By 2050, 68% of the world's population is projected to live in urban areas.

articles.headline: Indian Scientists Achieve Breakthrough in Indigenous Vaccine Development for Neglected Tropical Disease
articles.explainer:
• Indian researchers announced a significant breakthrough in developing an indigenous vaccine for a prevalent neglected tropical disease (NTD).
• The vaccine candidate has shown promising results in pre-clinical trials, demonstrating high efficacy and safety profiles.
• This development is crucial for public health, as NTDs disproportionately affect vulnerable populations in low-income settings.
• The initiative aligns with India's 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' vision in healthcare and pharmaceutical innovation.
• Expedited clinical trials are expected, potentially leading to a cost-effective and accessible vaccine for global distribution.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: NTDs are a diverse group of communicable diseases prevalent in tropical and subtropical regions.
Examples of NTDs: Dengue, Chikungunya, Kala-azar (Visceral Leishmaniasis), Lymphatic Filariasis.
India has significantly reduced the burden of several NTDs.
Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council (BIRAC) supports such R&D.
Universal Immunization Programme (UIP) is a key public health initiative in India.
mainsPoints: Significance of indigenous vaccine development for India's public health security and reducing reliance on imports.
Challenges in combating NTDs: poverty, lack of access to healthcare, complex disease vectors.
Role of government funding, private sector collaboration, and international partnerships in vaccine research.
Impact of NTDs on economic productivity and human development in affected regions.
India's potential as a global pharmaceutical hub ('Pharmacy of the World') for affordable medicines and vaccines.
Ethical considerations in vaccine development and equitable distribution.
articles.imageDescription: Microscopic view of a vaccine vial with a blurred background of scientists working in a lab, symbolizing medical research.
articles.trivia: NTDs affect over a billion people globally, causing debilitating illness, disability, and poverty.

articles.headline: Government Considers New Market Intervention Scheme to Stabilize Prices for Key Agricultural Commodities
articles.explainer:
• The Union Agriculture Ministry is reportedly considering a new Market Intervention Scheme (MIS) to address price volatility for crucial agricultural commodities.
• The proposed scheme aims to provide a safety net for farmers by procuring produce at a pre-determined price when market prices fall below a certain threshold.
• Focuses on perishable commodities not covered by existing Minimum Support Price (MSP) mechanisms.
• Seeks to protect farmers' incomes, prevent distress sales, and ensure food security for consumers.
• Details of the scheme, including commodities covered and operational modalities, are under discussion with state governments.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, - different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers
Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System—objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security
Economics of animal-rearing
prelimsPoints: MSP is announced for 22 mandated crops and Fair and Remunerative Price (FRP) for sugarcane.
MIS is an ad-hoc scheme implemented on the request of State Governments for perishable horticultural/agricultural commodities.
Nodal agency for MIS is the Department of Agriculture, Cooperation and Farmers Welfare.
Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP) recommends MSPs.
Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY) is an crop insurance scheme.
mainsPoints: Critique of existing agricultural price support mechanisms (MSP, MIS) and their limitations.
Challenges faced by Indian farmers due to price volatility and market imperfections.
Impact of the proposed scheme on farmers' welfare, agricultural production patterns, and food inflation.
Role of government intervention in agricultural markets vs. free market principles.
Logistical challenges in procurement, storage, and distribution of perishable commodities.
Sustainable solutions for doubling farmers' income and ensuring long-term agricultural growth.
articles.imageDescription: Farmers sorting fresh produce in a market, with a graph showing fluctuating prices in the background, symbolizing market intervention.
articles.trivia: India is the world's second-largest producer of fruits and vegetables, but a significant portion is lost due to post-harvest issues.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Announces Major Restoration Project for Ancient Temple Complex in Karnataka
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has initiated a large-scale restoration project for a historically significant ancient temple complex in Karnataka.
• The multi-year project aims to preserve the intricate architecture, sculptures, and inscriptions of the temple, dating back to the Hoysala period.
• Involves collaboration with conservation experts, historians, and local communities to ensure authentic restoration techniques.
• Expected to boost cultural tourism in the region and provide insights into India's rich architectural heritage.
• The initiative underscores India's commitment to safeguarding its historical monuments and promoting cultural awareness.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Preservation of cultural heritage
prelimsPoints: ASI is the premier organization for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage in India.
Hoysala temples (e.g., Chennakeshava Temple at Belur, Hoysaleswara Temple at Halebidu) are known for intricate carvings and star-shaped plans.
Hoysala architecture is a distinctive Vesara style, blending Northern and Southern Indian temple forms.
Karnataka has numerous UNESCO World Heritage Sites, including Hampi and Pattadakal.
The Ancient Monuments and Archaeological Sites and Remains Act, 1958, governs monument protection.
mainsPoints: Significance of preserving ancient architectural heritage for national identity and cultural continuity.
Challenges in monument conservation: environmental degradation, urban encroachment, lack of funding, skilled labor.
Role of ASI and other organizations in archaeological research, excavation, and restoration.
Impact of cultural tourism on local economies and infrastructure development.
Debate on balancing preservation with public access and commercialization of heritage sites.
Technological advancements (e.g., 3D scanning, laser cleaning) in heritage conservation.
articles.imageDescription: Close-up of intricate stone carvings on a Hoysala temple wall, with scaffolding indicating ongoing restoration work.
articles.trivia: Hoysala temples are often described as 'poetic in stone' due to their exquisite and detailed sculptures.

articles.headline: CERT-In Issues High Alert on New Sophisticated Ransomware Attacks Targeting Critical Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) issued a high-severity alert regarding a new wave of sophisticated ransomware attacks.
• The attacks are specifically targeting critical infrastructure sectors, including energy, finance, and healthcare.
• The ransomware employs advanced evasion techniques and exploits previously unknown vulnerabilities.
• CERT-In advises organizations to immediately implement enhanced cybersecurity measures, including robust backups, network segmentation, and employee training.
• The alert underscores the growing threat of cyber warfare and the need for a resilient national cybersecurity posture.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
keyTerms: National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC)
prelimsPoints: CERT-In is the national agency for responding to computer security incidents.
National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC) protects critical information infrastructure.
Ransomware encrypts data and demands payment (ransom) for decryption.
The Information Technology Act, 2000, provides the legal framework for cybercrime.
India has a National Cyber Security Policy.
mainsPoints: Growing threat of cyberattacks (ransomware, state-sponsored hacking) on India's critical infrastructure.
Economic and national security implications of successful cyberattacks on key sectors.
Challenges in attribution and prosecution of cybercriminals operating across borders.
Strategies for enhancing India's cybersecurity resilience: policy, technology, human resources, international cooperation.
Role of public-private partnerships in securing digital assets.
Impact of emerging technologies (AI, IoT, 5G) on the cyber threat landscape.
articles.imageDescription: A digital lock icon overlaid on a network diagram, with a warning sign, symbolizing a cybersecurity threat.
articles.trivia: The global cost of cybercrime is projected to reach trillions of dollars annually in the coming years.

articles.headline: High Court Directs State Governments to Expedite Environmental Clearances, Balancing Development and Conservation
articles.explainer:
• A High Court issued a significant directive to several state governments, urging them to expedite the processing of environmental clearances for infrastructure projects.
• The court emphasized the need to strike a balance between developmental imperatives and environmental protection.
• Criticized delays in bureaucratic processes that hinder project implementation while upholding the sanctity of environmental norms.
• Mandated the establishment of fast-track mechanisms for clearances without compromising on scientific assessments.
• The ruling is expected to streamline project approvals while reinforcing adherence to environmental impact assessment (EIA) guidelines.

National Green Tribunal (NGT) deals with cases relating to environmental protection.
The 'Polluter Pays Principle' is a key concept in environmental law.
Expert Appraisal Committees (EAC) and State Expert Appraisal Committees (SEAC) review projects.
mainsPoints: Challenges in balancing economic development goals with environmental sustainability in India.

articles.headline: India-Sri Lanka Fisheries Dialogue Concludes with Focus on Sustainable Practices and Conflict Resolution
articles.explainer:
• A high-level bilateral dialogue between India and Sri Lanka on fisheries issues concluded, addressing long-standing disputes.
• Discussions focused on promoting sustainable fishing practices, protecting marine ecosystems, and ensuring the livelihoods of fishing communities.
• Both nations agreed on enhanced cooperation in patrolling, data sharing, and joint research to mitigate illegal fishing.
• A roadmap for resolving the issue of Indian fishermen allegedly straying into Sri Lankan waters was outlined, including alternative livelihood options.
• The dialogue aims to transform a contentious issue into a collaborative framework for shared marine resources.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: The Palk Strait separates India and Sri Lanka, a rich fishing ground.
The Katchatheevu Island, ceded to Sri Lanka in 1974, is a point of contention.
Bottom trawling, often used by Indian fishermen, is banned by Sri Lanka due to its ecological damage.
India has schemes like the 'Blue Revolution' and deep-sea fishing promotion.
India and Sri Lanka are members of BIMSTEC and SAARC.
mainsPoints: Historical context and evolution of the India-Sri Lanka fisheries dispute.
Economic, social, and environmental dimensions of the conflict, particularly for Tamil Nadu fishermen.
Diplomatic challenges in resolving transboundary resource disputes.
Viable solutions: deep-sea fishing promotion, alternative livelihoods, joint management of resources, technological solutions.
Impact of the dispute on bilateral relations and regional stability.
Role of international law and bilateral agreements in managing shared maritime boundaries.
articles.imageDescription: Fishing boats in a calm sea with the flags of India and Sri Lanka subtly merged, symbolizing cooperation.
articles.trivia: The Palk Strait is relatively shallow, with a depth of less than 9 meters in some areas, making it a challenging maritime boundary.

articles.headline: Global Summit on Digital Public Infrastructure Convenes in New Delhi, India Showcases UPI and Aadhaar Models
articles.explainer:
• New Delhi hosted a major international summit focused on Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI), with delegates from over 50 countries.
• India showcased its successful models of UPI (Unified Payments Interface) and Aadhaar as examples of scalable and inclusive DPI.
• Discussions centered on leveraging DPI for financial inclusion, governance, and accelerating sustainable development goals.
• The summit aimed to foster global collaboration in building open, interoperable, and secure digital ecosystems.
• India positioned itself as a leader in digital transformation, offering to share its expertise with developing nations.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
prelimsPoints: Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) refers to shared digital systems that are foundational for public and private service delivery.
India Stack is a set of APIs that allows governments, businesses, startups, and developers to utilize a unique digital infrastructure.
Aadhaar is a 12-digit unique identification number issued by UIDAI.
UPI is a real-time payment system developed by NPCI.
ONDC is an initiative to promote open networks for all aspects of exchange of goods and services over digital or electronic networks.
mainsPoints: Significance of India's DPI models (Aadhaar, UPI, ONDC) for financial inclusion and democratic governance.
Challenges and opportunities in scaling DPI in diverse global contexts, especially in developing countries.
Ethical considerations and data privacy concerns associated with large-scale digital identity systems.
Role of DPI in achieving Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) and fostering inclusive growth.
India's leadership in digital diplomacy and its 'Digital India' vision.
Potential for global standards and interoperability in DPI to create a 'digital commons'.
articles.imageDescription: A world map with digital lines connecting various countries, featuring icons of UPI and Aadhaar, symbolizing global DPI collaboration.
articles.trivia: UPI transactions in India surpassed 10 billion per month in 2023, making it one of the world's most successful real-time payment systems.

articles.headline: Global Report Highlights India as a Major Hotspot for Internal Climate Migration by 2050
articles.explainer:
• A new global report on climate change and migration identified India as one of the countries expected to experience significant internal climate migration by 2050.
• The report projects millions of people being displaced internally due to rising sea levels, extreme heat, water scarcity, and agricultural stress.
• It emphasizes the need for proactive policies, urban planning, and adaptation strategies to manage the demographic shift.
• Calls for international cooperation and funding to support vulnerable communities and build resilience.
• Highlights the interconnectedness of climate change, poverty, and human mobility, urging integrated policy responses.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
Population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies
prelimsPoints: Climate migration refers to people forced to leave their homes due to climate change impacts.
Internal migration within India is already significant, driven by economic and environmental factors.
Coastal areas, arid regions, and flood-prone river basins are particularly vulnerable.
IPCC reports consistently highlight climate change as a driver of migration.
India's National Action Plan on Climate Change (NAPCC) includes missions for adaptation.
mainsPoints: Socio-economic impacts of large-scale internal climate migration on source and destination regions.
Challenges in managing climate migrants: housing, employment, social integration, infrastructure strain.
Policy interventions needed: climate-resilient agriculture, water management, early warning systems, planned relocation.
Role of international cooperation and climate finance in addressing climate-induced displacement.
Ethical and human rights dimensions of climate migration, particularly for vulnerable groups.
Linkages between climate change, food security, and livelihood stability in India.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized map of India showing arrows indicating population movement, overlaid with symbols of floods, droughts, and heatwaves.
articles.trivia: A World Bank report in 2021 estimated that by 2050, 216 million people could become internal climate migrants globally.

highlights: India launches National Green Hydrogen Mission 2.0 with ambitious targets, aiming for global leadership in green energy.
Supreme Court upholds Women's Reservation Bill, clearing path for 33% reservation in legislatures after delimitation.
India and Japan forge strategic partnership on critical minerals and supply chain resilience, enhancing economic security.
ISRO successfully tests next-generation Reusable Launch Vehicle prototype, boosting India's space capabilities.
Project Cheetah achieves milestone with new cubs born at Kuno National Park, indicating successful adaptation and breeding.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. The mission aims to make India a global hub for the production, utilization, and export of green hydrogen.
2. The Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition (SIGHT) Programme is a component of the mission, focusing on electrolyser manufacturing and green hydrogen production.
3. The Ministry of Power is the nodal ministry for the implementation of this mission.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct as the mission aims to position India as a global leader in green hydrogen. Statement 2 is correct as SIGHT is a key component to incentivize domestic manufacturing and production. Statement 3 is incorrect; the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy is the nodal ministry for the National Green Hydrogen Mission.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about the Women's Reservation Bill (Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam) is/are correct?
1. It provides for 33% reservation for women in the Lok Sabha, State Legislative Assemblies, and the Delhi Assembly.
2. Its implementation is contingent upon the completion of the next census and subsequent delimitation exercise.
3. The reservation is initially for a period of 10 years, extendable by parliamentary law.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. Statement 2 is correct, as implementation is linked to census and delimitation. Statement 3 is incorrect; the reservation is initially for 15 years, not 10 years, and can be extended by Parliament.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following pairs regarding initiatives related to critical minerals:
1. India-Japan Strategic Partnership: Focus on joint exploration and processing of critical minerals.
2. Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme: Incentivizing Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) battery manufacturing.
3. Project Cheetah: Aims at identifying new domestic reserves of rare earth elements.
How many of the above pairs are correctly matched?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Pair 1 is correctly matched, as India and Japan are collaborating on critical minerals. Pair 2 is correctly matched, as the PLI scheme promotes ACC battery manufacturing, which uses critical minerals. Pair 3 is incorrectly matched; Project Cheetah is for the reintroduction of cheetahs, not for mineral exploration.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for publishing the Financial Stability Report (FSR) in India?
options: (b) Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI)
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Financial Stability Report (FSR) is a semi-annual publication of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI). It reflects the collective assessment of the Sub-Committee of the Financial Stability and Development Council (FSDC) on risks to financial stability.

prelimsMocks.question:
With respect to the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) in India, which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. Article 44 of the Indian Constitution mandates the State to secure a Uniform Civil Code for all citizens.
2. Goa is the only state in India that currently has a Uniform Civil Code.
3. The Shah Bano case verdict was a landmark judgment related to the implementation of the UCC.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct; Article 44 is a DPSP on UCC. Statement 2 is correct; Goa inherited a UCC from the Portuguese Civil Code. Statement 3 is correct; the Shah Bano case (1985) brought the debate on UCC to the forefront regarding maintenance for Muslim women.

mainsMocks.question: Discuss the strategic imperatives behind India's National Green Hydrogen Mission 2.0. What are the key challenges and opportunities in positioning India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export?

Conclude with opportunities like job creation, export potential, and India's renewable energy advantage.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's upholding of the Women's Reservation Bill marks a significant step towards inclusive democracy. mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments; Parliament and State Legislatures; Role of women and women’s organization.
mainsMocks.approach: Briefly mention the Women's Reservation Bill and the Supreme Court's ruling. Discuss the positive impacts on political representation, gender justice, policy-making, and women's empowerment. Identify implementation challenges such as delimitation, potential resistance, and the debate around 'quota within quota'. Conclude with the importance of political will for effective implementation.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Announces Major Restoration Project for Ancient Temple Complex in Karnataka
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has initiated a large-scale restoration project for a historically significant ancient temple complex in Karnataka.
• The multi-year project aims to preserve the intricate architecture, sculptures, and inscriptions of the temple, dating back to the Hoysala period.
• Involves collaboration with conservation experts, historians, and local communities to ensure authentic restoration techniques.
• Expected to boost cultural tourism in the region and provide insights into India's rich architectural heritage.
• The initiative underscores India's commitment to safeguarding its historical monuments and promoting cultural awareness.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Preservation of cultural heritage
prelimsPoints: ASI is the premier organization for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage in India.
Hoysala temples (e.g., Chennakeshava Temple at Belur, Hoysaleswara Temple at Halebidu) are known for intricate carvings and star-shaped plans.
Hoysala architecture is a distinctive Vesara style, blending Northern and Southern Indian temple forms.
Karnataka has numerous UNESCO World Heritage Sites, including Hampi and Pattadakal.
The Ancient Monuments and Archaeological Sites and Remains Act, 1958, governs monument protection.
mainsPoints: Significance of preserving ancient architectural heritage for national identity and cultural continuity.
Challenges in monument conservation: environmental degradation, urban encroachment, lack of funding, skilled labor.
Role of ASI and other organizations in archaeological research, excavation, and restoration.
Impact of cultural tourism on local economies and infrastructure development.
Debate on balancing preservation with public access and commercialization of heritage sites.
Technological advancements (e.g., 3D scanning, laser cleaning) in heritage conservation.
GS1.imageDescription: Close-up of intricate stone carvings on a Hoysala temple wall, with scaffolding indicating ongoing restoration work.
GS1.trivia: Hoysala temples are often described as 'poetic in stone' due to their exquisite and detailed sculptures.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Validity of Key Provisions in Women's Reservation Bill, Paving Way for Implementation
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, affirmed the constitutional validity of the provisions related to the Women's Reservation Bill.
• The judgment clarifies the modalities and timelines for the implementation of 33% reservation for women in Lok Sabha and state assemblies.
• Emphasizes the importance of inclusive political representation for strengthening democratic processes.
• Directs the Election Commission and relevant ministries to prepare for the necessary logistical and administrative changes.
• The ruling resolves legal ambiguities, setting a clear path for the historic legislative reform.

prelimsPoints: The Women's Reservation Bill (Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam) was passed as the 128th Constitutional Amendment Bill.
Provides for 33% reservation for women in Lok Sabha, state legislative assemblies, and the Delhi assembly.
Implementation is linked to the delimitation exercise following the next census.
The reservation will be for a period of 15 years, with the possibility of extension.
Articles 330A, 332A, and 334A were inserted/amended.
mainsPoints: Significance of women's reservation for empowering women and transforming political discourse.

GS2.headline: India and Japan Forge Comprehensive Strategic Partnership on Critical Minerals and Supply Chain Resilience
GS2.explainer:
• India and Japan signed a landmark agreement to establish a comprehensive strategic partnership focused on critical minerals and resilient supply chains.
• The pact aims to reduce dependence on single-source nations for key minerals essential for advanced technologies and green energy transition.
• Includes joint investments in exploration, processing, and recycling of critical minerals such as lithium, cobalt, and rare earths.
• Both nations commit to sharing technological expertise and developing sustainable supply chain practices.
• This collaboration is seen as a significant step towards enhancing economic security and strategic autonomy in the Indo-Pacific region.

Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests

Examples: Lithium (EV batteries), Cobalt (batteries), Rare Earth Elements (electronics, magnets).
India has identified 30 critical minerals for its economic development.
Japan is a major technology producer reliant on stable mineral supply.
Partnership aims to diversify sources and reduce geopolitical risks in supply chains.
mainsPoints: Significance of critical minerals for India's 'Make in India' initiative and energy transition goals.
Challenges posed by global concentration of critical mineral processing and mining.
How this partnership strengthens India's strategic autonomy and reduces vulnerability.
Role of India-Japan cooperation in balancing geopolitical influence in the Indo-Pacific.
Potential for technological transfer and joint R&D in mineral extraction and processing.
Economic implications: boosting manufacturing, creating jobs, and fostering innovation.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan superimposed over an illustration of various critical minerals and electronic components.
GS2.trivia: India's first critical minerals auction for 20 blocks, including lithium and rare earth elements, was launched in 2023.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Overhaul of Disaster Management Framework Amidst Rising Extreme Weather Events
GS2.explainer:
• A parliamentary standing committee submitted a comprehensive report recommending significant reforms in India's disaster management framework.
• The report highlights gaps in preparedness, early warning systems, and post-disaster rehabilitation efforts, especially for extreme weather events.
• Advocates for a more decentralized approach, empowering local bodies and enhancing community resilience.
• Suggests increased funding for disaster-resistant infrastructure and climate-resilient agriculture.
• Emphasizes the integration of climate change adaptation strategies into national and state disaster management plans.

NDMA (National Disaster Management Authority) is chaired by the Prime Minister.

The 15th Finance Commission recommended allocating funds for disaster risk reduction and mitigation.
mainsPoints: Critical analysis of India's current disaster management structure and its effectiveness.

Challenges in coordination between central, state, and local agencies during disasters.
Recommendations for building a truly resilient nation against natural calamities.

GS2.trivia: India has a unique geographical vulnerability, prone to over 30 types of natural disasters.

GS2.headline: NEP 2020 Implementation Review Focuses on Enhancing Vocational Education Integration for Skill Development
GS2.explainer:
• A mid-term review of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation highlighted significant progress in integrating vocational education.
• The review emphasized the need for further strengthening industry-academia linkages and skill development initiatives.
• Recommendations include expanding vocational courses in schools and higher education institutions, aligning with market demands.
• Focus is on making vocational education an integral part of mainstream schooling, starting from Class 6.
• Aims to equip India's youth with job-ready skills, reducing unemployment and fostering entrepreneurship.

• The exercise focused on enhancing interoperability, maritime domain awareness, and coordinated responses to regional security challenges.
• Scenarios included anti-piracy operations, humanitarian assistance and disaster relief (HADR), and anti-submarine warfare.

GS2.headline: Discussions on Uniform Civil Code (UCC) Gain Momentum as Legal Committee Submits Recommendations
GS2.explainer:
• The legal committee constituted to examine the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) submitted its detailed recommendations to the government.
• The report outlines potential frameworks for a common set of laws governing personal matters like marriage, divorce, inheritance, and adoption.
• The recommendations aim to harmonize diverse personal laws currently applicable to different religious communities in India.
• Public debate on the UCC has intensified, with various stakeholders expressing views on its implications for secularism and individual rights.
• The government is expected to review the recommendations and consider legislative action, potentially leading to a significant social reform.

Role of international law and bilateral agreements in managing shared maritime boundaries.

GS2.trivia: The Palk Strait is relatively shallow, with a depth of less than 9 meters in some areas, making it a challenging maritime boundary.

GS3.headline: India Unveils National Green Hydrogen Mission 2.0 with Ambitious Targets for Decarbonization and Energy Security
GS3.explainer:
• Phase 2.0 builds on the initial mission, setting enhanced targets for green hydrogen production capacity by 2030.
• Focuses on expanding domestic manufacturing of electrolysers and promoting green hydrogen adoption across key industrial sectors like refining, fertilizers, and steel.
• Includes new financial incentives, R&D support, and development of green hydrogen corridors and export infrastructure.
• Aims to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy independence and climate goals.
• Emphasizes skill development and job creation in the emerging green hydrogen economy.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: Energy
Environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
GS3.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched in 2023.
Initial target for green hydrogen production: 5 MMT by 2030.
SIGHT (Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition) Programme is a key component.
Key applications include fertilizers, refineries, steel, and heavy mobility.
Nodal Ministry: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy.
mainsPoints: Significance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and reducing import bill.
Potential for decarbonizing hard-to-abate industrial sectors.
Challenges include high production cost, infrastructure development, and water availability.
Government's role in creating an enabling policy framework, incentives, and R&D support.
India's strategic advantage due to abundant renewable energy resources.
Geopolitical implications: positioning India as a global energy exporter and leader in green technologies.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration showing a futuristic green hydrogen production plant with solar panels and wind turbines in the background.
GS3.trivia: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity, resulting in zero greenhouse gas emissions.

GS3.headline: RBI Releases Financial Stability Report, Cautions on Global Headwinds Amidst Domestic Resilience
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) published its semi-annual Financial Stability Report (FSR), assessing the health of India's financial system.
• The report highlighted the continued resilience of the banking sector, with strong capital buffers and improved asset quality.
• It cautioned about potential global headwinds, including geopolitical tensions, commodity price volatility, and slower global growth, which could impact domestic stability.
• Emphasized the need for ongoing vigilance and robust risk management frameworks across financial institutions.
• Noted the steady growth in credit and deposits, signaling healthy economic activity, but also flagged concerns regarding rising household debt in certain segments.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
Financial sector reforms
prelimsPoints: Financial Stability Report (FSR) is published by RBI twice a year.
It reflects the collective assessment of the Sub-Committee of the Financial Stability and Development Council (FSDC) on risks to financial stability.
Key indicators include Capital to Risk-weighted Assets Ratio (CRAR), Gross Non-Performing Assets (GNPA) ratio.
FSR covers commercial banks, cooperative banks, NBFCs, and other financial institutions.
FSDC is chaired by the Union Finance Minister.
mainsPoints: Role of RBI in maintaining financial stability and macro-economic management.
Interlinkages between global economic trends and India's domestic financial health.
Challenges posed by rising household debt and implications for financial inclusion.
Importance of regulatory oversight and stress testing for banking sector resilience.
Impact of digital transformation and fintech on financial stability and evolving risks.
Government and RBI coordination in addressing systemic risks and promoting inclusive growth.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic representation of financial stability, with upward trending graphs and a strong banking sector.
GS3.trivia: The Financial Stability and Development Council (FSDC) was set up in 2010 to strengthen and institutionalize the mechanism for maintaining financial stability.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Key Test of Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle Prototype
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved a significant milestone by successfully conducting a crucial test of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype.
• The test involved a precise landing experiment, demonstrating autonomous navigation, guidance, and control capabilities during re-entry.
• This technology is vital for reducing the cost of space access and enabling more frequent space missions.
• The RLV project aims to develop a two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) fully reusable launch system.
• The successful test brings India closer to developing its own space shuttle-like capabilities, bolstering its position in global space exploration.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
prelimsPoints: ISRO's RLV-TD (Reusable Launch Vehicle - Technology Demonstrator) program aims to develop reusable launch vehicles.
RLV-TD has already demonstrated hypersonic flight and autonomous landing capabilities.
It is designed to reduce the cost of launching satellites significantly.
Other countries with RLV capabilities include USA (SpaceX, NASA) and China.
Gaganyaan mission aims for human spaceflight using indigenous technology.
mainsPoints: Significance of reusable launch vehicles for India's future space missions and commercial space industry.
Economic benefits: reducing launch costs, increasing launch frequency, and enabling new space applications.
Technological challenges in developing RLVs: thermal protection systems, autonomous guidance, propulsion.
India's growing role in the global space market and its strategic implications.
Linkage with 'Make in India' and self-reliance in critical space technologies.
Potential for international collaborations and partnerships in space exploration.
GS3.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle prototype descending for a landing, with the ISRO logo.
GS3.trivia: The RLV-TD is designed to act as a flying testbed to evaluate various technologies, including hypersonic flight, autonomous landing, and winged re-entry.

GS3.headline: Project Cheetah Success: New Cheetah Cubs Born at Kuno National Park, Boosting Conservation Efforts
GS3.explainer:
• Kuno National Park has reported the birth of several new cheetah cubs, marking a significant milestone for Project Cheetah.
• The births indicate successful adaptation and breeding of the reintroduced cheetahs in their new habitat.
• This development is crucial for establishing a viable and self-sustaining cheetah population in India.
• Conservationists highlight the intensive monitoring and habitat management efforts contributing to this success.
• The project aims to restore cheetahs to India's ecosystem, enhancing biodiversity and ecological balance.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity and its conservation
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Project Cheetah was launched in 2022 to reintroduce cheetahs to India after their extinction in 1952.
Cheetahs were translocated from Namibia and South Africa to Kuno National Park in Madhya Pradesh.
Kuno National Park was chosen due to its suitable habitat and prey base.
Cheetahs are listed as 'Vulnerable' on the IUCN Red List.
India is the only country with lions, tigers, and cheetahs in its wildlife.
mainsPoints: Ecological significance of cheetah reintroduction for grassland ecosystems and predator-prey dynamics.
Challenges faced by Project Cheetah: adaptation, mortality rates, human-wildlife conflict.
Importance of community involvement and local support for conservation projects.
Role of scientific monitoring and adaptive management in wildlife reintroduction programs.
Comparison with other wildlife conservation efforts in India (e.g., Project Tiger, Project Elephant).
Ethical considerations and long-term sustainability of transboundary wildlife transfers.
GS3.imageDescription: Photograph of a mother cheetah with her cubs in a grassy field at Kuno National Park.
GS3.trivia: The cheetah is the fastest land animal, capable of reaching speeds of up to 120 km/h over short distances.

GS3.headline: Government Announces New PLI Scheme for Advanced Battery Manufacturing to Boost EV Ecosystem
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government launched a new Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme specifically for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) battery manufacturing.
• The scheme aims to attract significant investments in the domestic manufacturing of high-efficiency batteries for electric vehicles (EVs) and energy storage.
• Offers financial incentives based on sales of batteries manufactured in India, promoting local value addition.
• Expected to reduce India's import dependence for batteries and foster a robust EV ecosystem.
• This initiative aligns with India's climate commitments and goal of achieving Net-Zero emissions by 2070.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Industrial policy and its effects on industrial growth
Infrastructure: Energy
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: The PLI scheme was launched to boost domestic manufacturing in various sectors.
ACC batteries are crucial for electric vehicles and grid-scale energy storage.
India aims for 30% EV penetration in private cars, 70% in commercial vehicles, and 80% in two-wheelers by 2030.
NITI Aayog's 'India's Electric Mobility Transformation' report highlights the need for indigenous battery production.
The FAME India Scheme promotes the adoption of electric and hybrid vehicles.
mainsPoints: Significance of indigenous battery manufacturing for India's energy security and reducing import bill.
Impact of PLI scheme on attracting foreign and domestic investment in the EV sector.
Challenges in battery manufacturing: raw material sourcing, technological advancements, recycling infrastructure.
Role of EVs in achieving India's climate change targets and reducing urban pollution.
Economic benefits: job creation, technological innovation, and export potential.
Government's comprehensive strategy for promoting electric mobility (PLI, FAME, charging infrastructure).
GS3.imageDescription: A sleek electric car being charged, with battery cells and manufacturing units in the background, symbolizing advanced battery production.
GS3.trivia: India is the third-largest automotive market in the world, making the transition to EVs a massive undertaking.

GS3.headline: New Policy Framework for Sustainable Urban Development and Green Infrastructure Unveiled
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs launched a new comprehensive policy framework aimed at promoting sustainable urban development.
• The policy emphasizes green infrastructure, smart city solutions, and climate-resilient urban planning.
• Includes guidelines for integrating renewable energy, efficient waste management, and water conservation into urban projects.
• Provides financial incentives and technical assistance to states and urban local bodies for adopting sustainable practices.
• Seeks to address rapid urbanization challenges while ensuring ecological balance and improving quality of life for city dwellers.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
GS3.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: Smart Cities Mission was launched in 2015 to promote sustainable and inclusive cities.
AMRUT (Atal Mission for Rejuvenation and Urban Transformation) focuses on urban infrastructure.
Swachh Bharat Mission (Urban) aims for universal sanitation coverage.
India's urban population is projected to reach over 600 million by 2030.
Green buildings use less water, optimize energy efficiency, conserve natural resources.
mainsPoints: Challenges of rapid urbanization in India: housing, sanitation, transport, pollution.
Importance of green infrastructure (e.g., urban forests, permeable pavements) in mitigating climate change impacts.
Role of technology and data analytics in developing smart and sustainable cities.
Financial and governance challenges in implementing large-scale urban development projects.
Impact of sustainable urban planning on public health, economic growth, and social equity.
Comparison with international best practices in sustainable urban design.
GS3.imageDescription: A modern, green cityscape with parks, renewable energy installations, and efficient public transport, symbolizing sustainable urban development.
GS3.trivia: By 2050, 68% of the world's population is projected to live in urban areas.

GS3.headline: Indian Scientists Achieve Breakthrough in Indigenous Vaccine Development for Neglected Tropical Disease
GS3.explainer:
• Indian researchers announced a significant breakthrough in developing an indigenous vaccine for a prevalent neglected tropical disease (NTD).
• The vaccine candidate has shown promising results in pre-clinical trials, demonstrating high efficacy and safety profiles.
• This development is crucial for public health, as NTDs disproportionately affect vulnerable populations in low-income settings.
• The initiative aligns with India's 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' vision in healthcare and pharmaceutical innovation.
• Expedited clinical trials are expected, potentially leading to a cost-effective and accessible vaccine for global distribution.
